Account: Margaret O'Leary, 12 years with you. I noticed my monthly loyalty discount of £5.00 was removed on the bill issued 22/03/2026. This is the first time I have contacted you about it. Please restore the discount and credit £5.00 to my account immediately, and confirm when this has been done.

Ticket INC12345678 was opened on 5 March regarding a broadband installation for my shop. I have called three times (8 March, 12 March, 19 March) and have not received a substantive progress update since the initial confirmation. The lack of communication is impeding my planning. I have exhausted the standard escalation steps listed on your site and now request immediate acknowledgement of the outstanding actions, a revised completion date, and to be put through to a manager if you cannot provide that information within three working days. If there is no response I will take the next step with the ombudsman.

I switched from another provider last month expecting a smoother experience and instead spent 42 minutes on hold when I first tried to call on 18 March. As a new customer this felt odd; I accepted the transfer in good faith but then had to abandon the call because of work. I am raising this for the first time because it seems counterintuitive that new customers should face long queues while establishing services. Please explain why the waiting time was so long and provide an alternative contact method or a guaranteed callback window so I do not have to wait on hold again.

So, it appears "fixed-term" contracts are now open to interpretation. My contract started on 10 October 2025 after I switched from another provider; I received an email dated 15 February 2026 informing me of a 12 per cent price increase taking effect from my next bill. That increase was not part of the terms I agreed to and it was implemented mid-contract on the bill dated 01 March 2026 — my monthly payment has risen from £42.00 to £47.04. I have called three times (18 February, 22 February and 04 March), used online chat twice and received standard responses that do not address the contractual point. Case references: 884512, 889003. Representatives I spoke with included Sam and Nina; none offered a workable resolution. I have exhausted your regular channels and therefore request escalation to a manager. If you cannot reinstate the original price or provide an equivalent remedy, please advise how to proceed with the Ombudsman Services: Communications.

I attempted to cancel my mobile plan today, 24 March 2026, and the experience has been absurdly time-consuming given how straightforward this should be. I’m a busy consultant and I booked time specifically to sort this: I logged into the app, went to Account > Manage Plan, clicked Cancel, and the page froze with error 503. I cleared cache, tried Chrome and Safari, and even used an incognito window. Same error and no confirmation email. I then used the web chat and explained I wanted a cancellation effective 31 March; the chat produced a bot response that asked for my last bill amount and then timed out. I called your customer line and waited 38 minutes before the call dropped. I have the call-time and the chat transcript saved. I am tech-savvy enough to know when a system is failing and where the failure sits — this looks like a backend routing problem, not user error. I do not want to be billed beyond 31 March. If the cancellation cannot be processed immediately via the account portal I expect a human to confirm it within 48 hours and a written confirmation sent to my email with a termination date and final bill estimate. If you need any further technical details (error logs, timestamps, browser versions), I will provide them, but I expect this resolved quickly; I have not had time to chase this repeatedly and will escalate to regulatory complaint channels if I am billed for the next month without proof of attempted cancellation on your systems.

I switched to your service less than a month ago and had expected at least basic accessibility to your support. Instead, what I have experienced is an absurdly long and unpredictable telephone queue that is already costing me time and money. On 24 March 2026 I rang at 09:17 to query an installation slot and was told the wait would be around 20 minutes; I was held for 42 minutes before the line cut out. At 16:03 on 25 March I called again and the IVR loop replayed the same options for ten minutes before a message announced the expected wait was 45–60 minutes; I waited 51 minutes and abandoned the call. I then used web chat on 25 March at 18:12 and stayed connected for 35 minutes answering automated prompts, only to be disconnected without explanation. The pattern is the same: long waits, repeated disconnections, no human contact when promised. This matters because the missed appointments have left my household without broadband during work hours and disrupted a contractor's installation visit. I accept that occasional delays happen, but the total lack of reliable access to support is unacceptable for a paying customer. Please investigate what is causing these queue failures, provide an explanation of the steps you will take to reduce wait times, and arrange a guaranteed callback within three working days at a time I specify. I also expect a goodwill gesture for lost working time and the cost of rearranged appointments. If you cannot arrange a callback, explain why and give me the name and contact details of someone senior who will take responsibility for resolving this.

My business is being impacted and I need this resolved immediately. I operate a small graphic design studio (Bright Oak Creative Ltd, account 008765) and contracted your Business Plus 100 service on 01/09/2025. We are relocating premises and requested formal cancellation to take effect 01/04/2026 because the new site will not be ready for another supplier until May. On 08/03/2026 I phoned support and spoke with Mark in the commercial retention team who said he would process a pro-rata refund for the unused month and issue a cancellation confirmation by email. That confirmation never arrived. Since then invoices INV-2026-03 and INV-2026-04 have been generated and payment was taken on 20/03/2026 (ref: PAY-3321), despite my instruction to cancel.

To be explicit: I require written confirmation that the contract will terminate on 01/04/2026, an itemised refund for the period billed after that date, and a return of any equipment collection instructions. This service is essential to contacting clients and invoicing; the continued billing and the ambiguity over cancellation threaten cashflow and client relationships. If you cannot supply the requested documentation and refund within seven calendar days, I will seek independent advice and consider formally disputing the charges and instructing a solicitor for breach of contract. Please respond with the cancellation reference number and refund timetable.

I have been a customer since 2012 and am disappointed that I am once again having to ask for a cancellation to be actioned. I requested termination of my broadband and line rental on 4 March via your telephone team and then followed up with an online chat on 11 March where I spoke with Michael, who confirmed that the request had been logged and that the final disconnection date would be 28 March. As of today there is no confirmation on my account, the service is still active and the online portal continues to show the monthly charge. I have been loyal to your company for more than a decade and am seeking a full explanation of why the cancellation has not been processed, together with an immediate confirmation of the disconnection date and a final bill showing any outstanding sums. Please escalate to a supervisor if necessary and email me the outcome; I appreciate that administrative errors happen but I would prefer this be resolved without further delay.

Family Home 350 plan: evening speeds have dropped to 8–14Mbps since 06/03/2026. Wired speedtests at 20:30 show 12Mbps. Rebooted hub and checked cables. Children cannot do homework with this. What are you going to do about restoring the advertised throughput?

I bought my handset on a student plan less than eight months ago and it has developed persistent faults that make it unreliable for day-to-day use. Over the past four weeks I have contacted support on 4 March, 11 March, 18 March and most recently 23 March; case numbers are 550124, 552391, 556708 and 559002. I spoke with advisors called Tom, Aisha and then two different repair agents; each time I was told a software reset or a collection would be arranged, but the problems remain. The phone's microphone cuts out during calls, the touch screen intermittently becomes unresponsive and the battery discharges from full to 20% within a few hours, which is not acceptable on a student budget. I cannot afford an out-of-warranty repair fee (quoted at around £75 when I asked), and repeated troubleshooting has not fixed the device. I would be grateful for a prompt solution: either an immediate replacement handset under the contract warranty or a prepaid collection for diagnostics and free repair. Please confirm next steps and a firm timescale within three working days so I can plan around my studies; I am disappointed this has taken several contacts without a durable outcome.

I've been with your company for 12 years and this is the first time I've felt I needed to ask for formal escalation. On 2 March I noticed two SIM swaps and three outgoing premium-rate calls on my account which I did not authorise. I phoned customer services on 3 March and spoke with Rachel (ref 847392); she confirmed the account would be frozen and said fraud would investigate. On 9 March I spoke to Tom (ref 855101) who told me the freeze wasn't applied and that a manager would review. I have followed every step suggested by your agents and supplied copies of my ID and police reference 2023/4568, but the disputed charges of £182.40 remain on the bill and the account was active for two days after I reported the issue. I am asking for a manager-review and a full reversal of the fraudulent transactions, and if you cannot resolve this promptly please pass the matter to the ombudsman. I am disappointed that after a decade with you I have had to reach this point, but I want a clear timeline for resolution within the next five working days.

On Monday 16 March my handset started shutting down mid-call while I was on a delivery run. I run a small courier operation and that phone is my primary way to accept bookings, navigate, and confirm drops. By 18 March I had spoken to your agent Aisha, who logged fault reference 90217 and said a replacement would be sent; I have received nothing and the device now fails to hold a charge for more than 30 minutes. As a result I missed a scheduled commercial drop and lost a £120 job fee that I had agreed with a client that morning.

I raised the fault once already and was promised a loan handset; that did not happen. I need either an immediate replacement handset or a working loan device while mine is assessed, and I need a record of the lost income covered or reimbursed. Please advise how you will get a working device to me within the next two working days so I can resume operations.

I switched my home and phone service to your company from Sky under order reference 778811 on 14/02/2026. The installation appointment was booked for 17/02/2026 and the porting of my number was due to complete on that day. Since then there has been no substantive communication: the last update on the portal was 17/02/2026 and I have had two calls to your helpdesk (Kate on 23/02, case 778811-23K) and a complaints email reply on 03/03/2026 that offered no timeline. I have now exhausted the normal channels and require escalation to a manager or the Ombudsman if you cannot supply a firm plan immediately. Under the standards expected of new customer migrations, you are obliged to keep me informed of progress and any delays; your silence has left me unable to confirm the switch with my contacts, and I have lost an evening of work waiting for the engineer who did not arrive. Please provide within 48 hours: (1) a confirmed date and two-hour slot for installation and number porting, (2) a precise explanation for the delay since 17/02/2026 and why I received no interim updates, and (3) details of compensation you will offer for the time and inconvenience caused. If you fail to respond with a satisfactory plan, I will take this further via alternative dispute resolution channels.

I run a small catering business and purchased ten company handsets and a business line from you on 12 January 2026 (account 44-19-221). Since 05 March 2026 multiple handsets show repeated SIM errors and no network registration. I have called support on 06 March and 11 March, spoken to Mark and then to Lydia, and completed every diagnostic your scripted agents suggested. The faults have rendered three of my delivery drivers unable to take orders or update customers. I have documented loss of earnings totalling £1,240. I have now exhausted the normal complaint routes and the issue remains. Under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 the goods supplied must be fit for purpose and of satisfactory quality. These handsets are clearly not. I am therefore formally requesting immediate remedial action: either full replacement of faulty devices within five working days or a pro rata refund for devices that fail to network, plus compensation for demonstrable loss. If you do not escalate this to a senior manager and provide a binding timetable by close of business on 29 March 2026 I will refer the matter to the Ombudsman and consider legal remedies to recover my losses. This is not a negotiation; it is a statement of my rights and the impact on my livelihood.

I am writing on behalf of my family plan account (Family Share 30GB, account holder: Rebecca Hardy) to complain about the absence of meaningful updates. We reported the ongoing loss of evening data for multiple devices on 1 March, chased progress on 8 March and again on 16 March, and have received only automated messages and a single brief call stating the ticket had been ‘escalated’ (reference 987654321). This is the fourth time I have had to prompt you, and I am now formally requesting a substantive status report within 48 hours explaining: the cause of the failure, the remedial steps taken to date, an estimated completion date, and any compensation for the predictable disruption to the children’s schoolwork and my work calls. Under the Consumer Rights Act and Ofcom guidelines I expect transparent communication and timely fault resolution; please confirm receipt of this email and provide the requested update without further delay.
